visits.note: Patient P022 is a 55-year-old male with a history of type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia. He presents with symptoms of fatigue, polyuria, and polydipsia. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 140/90 mmHg and a BMI of 32. Labs show HbA1c of 8.1, BP_systolic of 140, BP_diastolic of 90, eGFR of 72, and LDL of 140. Medications include Metformin 1000mg daily and Lisinopril 20mg daily. Diagnoses include type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia. Symptoms include fatigue, polyuria, and polydipsia.

Patient P022 returns 8 months after the initial visit with worsening symptoms of fatigue and increasing frequency of urination. His blood pressure has decreased slightly to 130/85 mmHg. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 130/85 mmHg and a BMI of 32. Labs show HbA1c of 7.8, BP_systolic of 130, BP_diastolic of 85, eGFR of 68, and LDL of 130. Medications include Metformin 1000mg daily, Lisinopril 25mg daily, and Atorvastatin 20mg daily. Diagnoses include type 2 diabetes, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and chronic kidney disease. Symptoms include fatigue, polyuria, polydipsia, and nocturia.

Patient P022 returns 8 months after the second visit with stable symptoms of fatigue and polyuria. His blood pressure has increased to 145/95 mmHg. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 145/95 mmHg and a BMI of 32. Labs show HbA1c of 7.0, BP_systolic of 145, BP_diastolic of 95, eGFR of 60, and LDL of 120. Medications include Metformin 1500mg daily, Lisinopril 30mg daily, Atorvastatin 40mg daily, and amlodipine 10mg daily. Diagnoses include type 2 diabetes, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, chronic kidney disease, and coronary artery disease. Symptoms include fatigue, polyuria, polydipsia, nocturia, and chest pain.
